description: Eliminate hardcoded dev reload port duplication in build scripts by importing the constant from a single source of truth.

userStories.title: Import DEV_RELOAD_PORT constant in build scripts instead of hardcoding
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the dev reload port to be defined in exactly one place so that changing it doesn't require updating multiple files.
acceptanceCriteria: DEV_RELOAD_PORT is defined in exactly one file (scripts/dev-reload-constants.ts)
platform/browser-extension/build-side-panel.ts imports DEV_RELOAD_PORT from scripts/dev-reload-constants.ts instead of hardcoding '18515'
platform/browser-extension/build-extension.ts imports DEV_RELOAD_PORT from scripts/dev-reload-constants.ts instead of hardcoding '18515'
No literal '18515' string remains in any .ts file except the constant definition
The .replace() calls use String(DEV_RELOAD_PORT) to convert the number to a string
npm run build passes (production build still works with no OPENTABS_DEV)
userStories.notes:
This is a mechanical search-and-replace change.

In `platform/browser-extension/build-side-panel.ts` (line 26):
- Add import: `import { DEV_RELOAD_PORT } from '../../scripts/dev-reload-constants.ts';`
- Change `.replace('__DEV_RELOAD_PORT__', '18515')` to `.replace('__DEV_RELOAD_PORT__', String(DEV_RELOAD_PORT))`

In `platform/browser-extension/build-extension.ts` (line 57):
- Add import: `import { DEV_RELOAD_PORT } from '../../scripts/dev-reload-constants.ts';`
- Change `.replace('__DEV_RELOAD_PORT__', '18515')` to `.replace('__DEV_RELOAD_PORT__', String(DEV_RELOAD_PORT))`

Both build scripts run via `npx tsx`, which handles TypeScript imports natively — no compilation needed. The import path is relative from `platform/browser-extension/` up to `scripts/`.

Note: the `.ts` extension in the import path is needed because these scripts run under tsx, not through the tsc build pipeline. tsx resolves `.ts` extensions directly.

Verify the constant is NOT in the tsc project graph for the extension package (it shouldn't be — it's a standalone script imported only by other standalone scripts). If tsc complains, use the `.js` extension in the import instead, since tsx resolves both.

The JS client files (`src/dev/reload-client.js` and `src/dev/reload-background.js`) use the `__DEV_RELOAD_PORT__` placeholder which is replaced at build time — they do NOT need to change.
